name: Atlas Copco
note: Compressor and vacuum world leader with a service annuity on the installed base — arguably Sweden's finest business, and priced accordingly.

name: Epiroc
note: Mining-equipment duopoly with Sandvik; aftermarket is ~2/3 of revenue. Quality clear, price rarely gives a cushion.

name: Sandvik
note: Mining and machining tools with strong aftermarket, more cyclical than Epiroc — occasionally offered at fair prices in downturns.

name: Volvo
note: Trucks, construction equipment and a service network money can't quickly replicate. Cheap multiple reflects the cycle — normalize before judging.

name: Assa Abloy
note: Global lock leader; spec-in with architects and switching costs on access systems make a genuinely durable moat.

name: Alfa Laval
note: Heat transfer and separation niches with large installed base and service tail; solid, seldom cheap.

name: Hexagon
note: Measurement software and sensors with sticky enterprise customers; governance noise has periodically discounted a quality asset.

name: Evolution
note: Live-casino near-monopoly with staggering margins at a low multiple — the discount prices regulatory risk. Decide if that's in your circle.

name: Hemnet
note: Sweden's property-listing network effect — the closest thing to a toll bridge on the exchange. Wonderful; the price knows it.

name: Avanza
note: Low-cost savings platform with Sweden's most loyal customers; earnings swing with rates and trading activity.

name: Swedbank
note: Dominant Swedish/Baltic retail bank at a single-digit multiple and near-7% yield; history says buy banks when they're unloved.

name: Handelsbanken
note: The most conservative Nordic bank — decades of lower credit losses than peers. Modest growth, dependable capital returns.

name: SEB
note: Corporate-banking franchise among Nordic large caps; quality mid-pack between Swedbank's yield and SHB's caution.

name: EQT
note: Private-markets brand with long fee locked-in AUM; earnings lumpy with carried interest and fundraising cycles.

name: H&M
note: Global brand and scale, but fast fashion has no pricing power and fierce competition — a fair company, occasionally at a wonderful price.

name: Essity
note: Tissue and hygiene brands (Tork, TENA) people buy in every economy; steady compounding at an undemanding multiple.

name: AAK
note: Specialty vegetable fats co-developed into customers' recipes — switching costs hide inside chocolate and infant formula.

name: Thule
note: Premium outdoor-carrier brand with real pricing power in its niche; demand tracks the leisure cycle.

name: Telia
note: Yield is the whole story — capital-hungry infrastructure, regulated returns, little growth. Buffett owns toll bridges, not telecoms.

name: Tele2
note: Leaner than Telia with better capital discipline and Baltic growth; still a competitive utility at heart.

name: Saab
note: Sovereign-backed order book stretching a decade — a real moat, but the rearmament premium leaves no margin of safety.

name: Boliden
note: Well-run miner and smelter, but copper and zinc prices decide earnings — no pricing power means no Buffett moat.

name: Holmen
note: Forest, paperboard and hydro power — the same irreplaceable-asset logic as SCA you already own; consider overlap.

name: Securitas
note: Guarding is labor arbitrage with thin margins; the tech-security shift may lift quality, but the moat today is shallow.

name: ABB
note: Electrification and automation leader riding grid investment and robotics; a large installed base feeds service revenue. Quality improved markedly since the 2010s — the price has noticed.

name: Addtech
note: Decentralized serial acquirer of niche technical-trading companies with a superb capital-allocation record — Lifco's cousin. Wonderful machine, punishing multiple.

name: Industrivärden
note: Holding company owning Volvo, Sandvik, Handelsbanken, Essity, SCA and Ericsson at a NAV discount — note the heavy look-through overlap with what you already own directly.

name: Lifco
note: Serial acquirer of niche market leaders in dental, demolition tools and systems, run with famous discipline. Decades of compounding — rarely, if ever, offered cheap.

name: NIBE
note: Heat-pump leader with a real long-term electrification tailwind, but the post-2022 inventory bust showed how cyclical structural growth can be. Earnings still rebuilding.

name: SKF
note: World's largest bearing maker with a sticky aftermarket, facing capable rivals (Schaeffler, Timken) and cyclical end markets. Fair company; the price is at least fair too.
